steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Factor $7$ from the terms with $x$.
$7x^2-bx-6=7(x^2-\frac{b}{7}x)-6$
Half of $-\frac{b}{7}$ is $-\frac{b}{14}$.

steps.title: Balance the added square
steps.content:
Add and subtract $(\frac{b}{14})^2$ inside the parentheses.
$7(x-\frac{b}{14})^2-7(\frac{b}{14})^2-6$
Since $7\cdot\frac{b^2}{196}=\frac{b^2}{28}$, the expression is $7(x-\frac{b}{14})^2-6-\frac{b^2}{28}$.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: You can also choose a positive value such as $b=14$ and compare the original expression with each choice for several $x$-values. The matching choice has the same outputs each time. The answer is A.
